summary: Biometric App Lock bug-fix verified on web preview. Backend regression: 21/21 pytest tests PASSED (auth, /api/auth/me, services CRUD, availability with Italian TZ slots, bookings create/conflict/mine, admin bookings, admin clients search+patch must_pay_online/blacklisted, admin service create + RBAC, waitlist join + auto-notify on admin cancel, blacklist blocks booking, payment intent). Frontend: (a) admin login -> /admin/diary works, no LockScreen rendered on web; (b) customer register -> /home works; (c) profile screen shows biometric-switch with subtitle 'Disponibile solo su iOS/Android' as required; (d) clicking the switch on web does NOT crash and does NOT trigger a LockScreen; (e) page reload keeps app functional (no LockScreen); (f) auth-context exposes 'locked' state (verified in code, defaults false, gated by Platform.OS==='web' in bioAvailableAndEnabled); (g) LockScreen component exists at /app/frontend/src/components/lock-screen.tsx and is imported+rendered conditionally in /app/frontend/app/_layout.tsx. Zero console errors, zero page errors observed on landing, login, home, profile, and services screens. Fixed one flaky backend test (test_payment_intent now iterates over the next 14 days to find an open slot instead of hard-coding day+4, which failed on closed days).
critical_code_review_comments: auth-context.tsx bioAvailableAndEnabled() correctly guards Platform.OS==='web' -> returns false, so 'locked' can never become true on web. Good.
_layout.tsx AppContent gates rendering: `if (locked) return <LockScreen />;` else Stack. Clean single-source-of-truth approach.
profile.tsx Switch is bound to `disabled={!bioAvailable}`; on web bioAvailable stays false and the correct Italian subtitle 'Disponibile solo su iOS/Android' is shown. Verified visible in DOM.
unlock() on web auto-succeeds (setLocked(false); return true) which is the correct graceful fallback for physical-device-only APIs.
LockScreen useEffect auto-triggers unlock() on native only (Platform.OS !== 'web') - correct.
server.py still 692 lines (same as iter 1) - consider modularizing (non-blocking).
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_ customer via UI register (biotest_*@example.com) and reused TEST_ users/services from previous iteration. No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Biometric App Lock feature is fully working on web (gracefully disabled with Italian message, no crash, no lock screen). The actual Face ID/Touch ID prompt can only be validated on a real iOS/Android device - not possible in web preview. All previous regression flows (services, bookings, waitlist, admin, schedule, notifications, blacklist, payments) still pass. Backend tests: 21/21.
